result.area: Wave 4 item 21 — video render-audit + edge-probe at the final gate (video-capable Gemini judging the actual rendered clip / source+offsets), on survivors only.
result.current_tool_state:
Installed SDK is google-genai 2.10.0 (verified: `.venv/bin/pip`), the same package Context7 tracks as `/googleapis/python-genai` (docs snippets at v1.33; the video API shape is unchanged and consistent with the installed types). This is the modern `client.models.generate_content` path — NOT the deprecated Interactions API — and it fully supports clipped video.

VIDEO-BY-OFFSET (verified against the installed SDK): `types.VideoMetadata` fields are exactly `start_offset: Optional[str]`, `end_offset: Optional[str]`, `fps: Optional[float]` (Context7: fps defaults to 1.0, valid range (0.0, 24.0]). Offsets are duration STRINGS, e.g. "120s"/"360s". `types.Part` carries `file_data`, `inline_data`, `video_metadata`, and `media_resolution` (verified: Part.model_fields). A clipped-video Part = `types.Part(file_data=types.FileData(file_uri=<uri>, mime_type="video/mp4"), video_metadata=types.VideoMetadata(start_offset="120s", end_offset="360s", fps=1.0))`. VideoMetadata is only valid when the video is provided inline or via a file (Context7: "Part > videoMetadata").

INLINE vs FILES API: inline via `types.Part.from_bytes(data=..., mime_type="video/mp4")` (verified signature; also accepts `media_resolution=`) — use when total request < ~20MB (Google video-understanding page: switch to Files API above 20MB; hard inline ceiling ~100MB). Files API: `client.files.upload(file=path)` returns a `File` with `.uri` and `.state`; you MUST poll `client.files.get(name=...)` until `state == FileState.ACTIVE` before generate_content (Context7: FileState enum STATE_UNSPECIFIED/PROCESSING/ACTIVE/FAILED). Files are up to 2GB free / 20GB paid, retained 48h. `client.files` exposes upload/get/list/delete/download (verified).

TOKEN/COST (ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/video-understanding): video sampled at 1 fps; DEFAULT media resolution ≈ 258 tokens/frame + 32 tokens/s audio ≈ ~300 tokens/s; LOW (`types.MediaResolution.MEDIA_RESOLUTION_LOW`, verified enum) ≈ 66 tokens/frame + 32 audio ≈ ~100 tokens/s. Pricing 2026: gemini-2.5-flash $0.30/M input, $2.50/M output; flash-lite ~$0.10/M input. So a 240s clip: default ≈72k tok → ~$0.022 (flash) / ~$0.0072 (flash-lite); LOW ≈24k tok → ~$0.0072 (flash)/~$0.0024 (flash-lite) — brackets the doc's ~$0.015/clip. An 8s edge probe at LOW ≈ 800 video tok + prompt/output ≈ ~$0.0001–0.0004 — matches the doc's ~$0.0006/candidate. Supported formats include video/mp4 (what cut.py emits).
result.environment_feasibility: Fully feasible on darwin arm64 with the existing stack — no new native/CUDA/licensed deps. google-genai 2.10.0 is already installed; ffmpeg is present at /opt/homebrew/bin/ffmpeg (config.FFMPEG_BIN, verified). Files API upload + generate_content are pure HTTPS calls; nothing runs locally. The real constraints are cost/latency and rate limits, not installability: (1) survivors-only volume is ~1–8 clips/video, keeping spend in the doc's $0.01–0.30/video band; (2) a full-source Files upload + ACTIVE poll adds seconds of latency and 48h server retention — the edge-probe avoids upload entirely by cutting an 8s segment locally (cut.py build_cmd) and sending inline bytes (<20MB); (3) Groq has NO video input, so JUDGE_PROVIDER="groq" cannot serve this — the video judge must be Gemini-only regardless of JUDGE_PROVIDER, exactly as vision.py already is (vision.py:57 calls gemini_client directly, bypassing the llm_json/Groq seam). Use JUDGE_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite for cost or gemini-2.5-flash for quality. Free-tier flash-lite RPM limits argue for gating this behind an opt-in flag (default OFF, mirroring diarization).
result.existing_code_audit:
GREENFIELD for video judging — grep across backend/ for VideoMetadata/files.upload/video_metadata/start_offset/edge_probe/render_audit/starts_clean returns nothing.

gemini_client.py: `generate_json` (text+response_schema, thinking-off-then-on retry, backoff) and `generate_json_mm(system, parts, schema)` (multimodal — sends a list of `types.Part` as `contents`; helpers `image_part`=Part.from_bytes(image/jpeg) at :101 and `text_part` at :106). There is NO video Part helper, NO Files API upload, NO video_metadata, NO media_resolution. Adding a `generate_json_video` is a direct extension of the existing generate_json_mm config pattern (:111-142).

llm.py:59 `llm_json` — the provider seam — accepts system/user STRINGS only, no media; the Gemini branch calls `gemini_client.generate_json` (text). So the video judge must NOT route through llm_json; it calls a new gemini_client.generate_json_video directly (same choice vision.py made).

validate.py: `judge_clip` (:133) is text-only — clip_text + a visual_summary STRING — routed through llm_json with judge provider/model (:149-154). `JudgeVerdict` (:43) has boolean gates incl. `visuals_sufficient` (:98 instructs "you cannot see the frames — set TRUE unless…"). `validate_and_repair` (:668) is the per-candidate gate run on ALL candidates during assembly (__init__.py:192) BEFORE cutting. The asymmetric quote-verified kill gate (_verify_failure_reasons :224, confirm_kill :268) and W25-E advisory pattern for single_idea (:308 _flag_unverified_atomicity → warning only, never gate) are the template for adding video gates as ADVISORY.

Visual flow today: perceive.py → vision.py (keyframe IMAGE captions, Gemini) → Perception.visual_events → Structure.visual_summary(start,end) (models.py:177 — concatenated on-screen text overlapping the window), which is the `visual_summary` string fed to the text judge (__init__.py:193,254). The render-audit upgrades this from "described visuals" to "actually watch frames + hear audio."

Pipeline hook point (orchestrator.py:205-238): assemble_clips [judge gate] → write_run_artifacts → refine_clip_boundaries (Whisper — SHIFTS offsets AFTER the judge, :222-225) → _build_embed_clips → cut_clips(video_path, :231). `video_path` is downloaded when want_mm (during perceive, :170) or want_cut (:198-203). So the correct render-audit hook is AFTER refine_clip_boundaries on the final clips_spec survivors, where offsets == shipped offsets and video_path exists. cut.py:42 `build_cmd(src, start_s, dur_s, out)` + finalize_output are reusable to cut an 8s edge locally.

Eval: metrics.py:44 passes `visual_summary=""` to judge_clip (item 24's "pass real visual_summary" is an adjacent fix); metrics.py:313 already threads a spec's visual_summary — the surface for reporting new video-gate outcomes.
result.recommended_approach:
Two-tier, survivors-only, additive — do NOT touch the existing text judge/repair loop, and keep every programmatic gate (MLLM judges over-flag; VF-Eval caveat in the doc).

TIER 1 — EDGE PROBE FIRST (cheap, catches the F7 mid-sentence-audio blind spot). Add gemini_client.generate_json_video supporting inline bytes. For each survivor AFTER refine_clip_boundaries: cut first ~8s and last ~8s locally with cut.py build_cmd, read the mp4 bytes, send inline (Part.from_bytes video/mp4, media_resolution=LOW) with a tiny schema {starts_clean_audio, ends_clean_audio, first_words, last_words, evidence}. ~$0.0001–0.0004/clip, no Files upload. Programmatic + advisory: a False nudges the boundary snap to the nearest sentence/silence edge or flags 'starts_mid_sentence_audio' — never kills. This is the highest value-per-dollar piece; ship it standalone behind EDGE_PROBE_ENABLED.

TIER 2 — RENDER AUDIT on survivors (opt-in, default OFF like diarization). Upload the FULL source video ONCE via client.files.upload, poll files.get until ACTIVE, then ONE generate_content per survivor referencing file_data + VideoMetadata(start_offset/end_offset) — no re-upload per clip. media_resolution=LOW, fps=1.0. New VideoVerdict schema adds `starts_clean_audio`, `ends_clean_audio`, `visuals_referenced_are_visible` (+ short evidence). Wire results as WARNINGS and a small final_quality dock (mirror W25-E single_idea advisory at validate.py:308) — never override the asymmetric quote-verified text kill gate. Gemini-only (bypass llm_json; ignore JUDGE_PROVIDER=groq), model = new VIDEO_JUDGE_MODEL (default flash-lite).

Both call a new gemini_client.generate_json_video(system, parts, schema, media_resolution) that reuses the generate_json_mm thinking-off-then-on + _retry machinery. Surface new fields in eval/artifacts. Guardrail (doc-confirmed): this is a FINAL gate only — do not replace the transcript-anchored structure stages with whole-video VLM grounding.

open_questions: Hook ordering: audit AFTER refine_clip_boundaries guarantees audited offsets == shipped offsets, but in default OUTPUT_MODE='embed' with multimodal ON the source video IS downloaded (perceive.py:170) — confirm we don't force an extra download when want_mm is false and want_cut is false (edge-probe would need the source regardless).

Render-audit source strategy: whole-source Files upload once + per-clip offsets (cheaper upload, 1 ACTIVE poll) vs local-cut each clip then inline/upload (N handles, simpler retention). Recommend whole-source+offsets for the 240s audit, local-cut+inline for the 8s edge probe — confirm the full source (could be a long lecture) uploads within Files limits/latency budget.

With PRECISE_BOUNDARIES (Whisper) already refining supadata's synthesized word timestamps, how much residual mid-sentence-audio error remains? Measure whether the edge-probe adds signal over Whisper snapping before investing in Tier 2.

Gating strength: should any video gate ever KILL, or stay warning-only until judge-human κ≥0.4 is established (doc's calibration gate)? Recommend advisory-only initially, consistent with W25-E single_idea.

media_resolution LOW vs MEDIUM/HIGH tradeoff for reading on-screen text/equations in visuals_referenced_are_visible — LOW (~100 tok/s) may miss small text; may need MEDIUM for slide-heavy content, raising cost ~3x.

result.area: Wave 4 items 22-23 — genre detection ENSEMBLE (metadata > audio > transcript-stats > LLM) + reconciliation pass + entertainment adapter. Unblocks the "Calculus Rhapsody" parody (uqwC41RDPyg) that ships 0 clips because it is mis-detected as "lecture".
result.current_tool_state: yt-dlp 2026.6.9 INSTALLED. info_dict exposes `categories`, `tags`, and (when present) `artist/track/genre/album`. GROUNDED CHECK — real metadata-only `extract_info(url, download=False)` for uqwC41RDPyg ("Calculus Rhapsody") returns: categories=["Comedy"], artist/track/genre/album all null, tags include "rhapsody","queen","bohemian rhapsody","calculus","math". So `categories` IS the reliable non-lecture signal here (Comedy), but artist/track/genre are NULL for user-uploaded parodies → those music fields are UNRELIABLE; do not rely on them alone. || google-genai 2.10.0 INSTALLED (repo uses new SDK: `from google import genai`). Audio understanding confirmed current (Context7 /googleapis/python-genai): `types.Part.from_bytes(data=audio_bytes, mime_type='audio/mpeg'|'audio/wav'|'audio/pcm')` passed into `client.models.generate_content(model='gemini-2.5-flash', contents=[text, part])`; inline request cap ~20MB (short window is tiny); structured `response_schema` works alongside audio parts. The repo already does image+schema via generate_json_mm, so audio is a drop-in. || inaSpeechSegmenter (PyPI, ina-foss): install_requires = numpy, pandas, scikit-image, pyannote.core, matplotlib, Pyro4, pytextgrid, soundfile; on darwin adds UNPINNED `tensorflow` + `onnxruntime-silicon`(arm64). python_requires>=3.8, classifiers list 3.8–3.13 (so 3.12 nominally supported). tensorflow-metal GPU plugin 1.2.0 (Jan 2025) ships wheels ONLY for py3.9–3.11 (no 3.12) — CPU `tensorflow` arm64 wheel works but Metal GPU accel is dead on 3.12. || wtpsplit/SaT, parakeet-mlx, MFA, torchaudio.forced_align: not relevant to genre detection — no role in this area.
result.environment_feasibility: Apple Silicon darwin arm64, Python 3.12.13, clips/.venv already holds torch 2.12.1, numpy 2.2.6, scipy 1.18.0, google-genai 2.10.0, yt-dlp 2026.6.9. RECOMMENDED tiers are all zero-new-dep and Apple-Silicon-native: (1) metadata via existing yt-dlp (free; works even in fast/supadata mode via a lightweight extract_info(download=False) ~1-2s network call); (2) Gemini-audio music check via existing Gemini client (~1 flash-lite call, no native deps). inaSpeechSegmenter is INSTALLABLE in principle (arm64 markers + 3.12 classifier) but is a HEAVY/RISKY dep: it pulls UNPINNED tensorflow (~0.5-1GB) + Pyro4 (deprecated) + pyannote.core + soundfile into the SHARED venv that already has torch 2.12 + numpy 2.2.6 — high risk of protobuf/numpy resolver churn and breaking torch/google-genai; Metal GPU wheel doesn't exist for 3.12 (CPU-only inference). RECOMMEND AGAINST adopting it; a Gemini-audio classify gives the same music-vs-speech discrimination (singing tagged as non-speech) for the cost of one LLM call and zero venv risk. Diarization tier (item 22's interview-vs-story turn stats) is UNAVAILABLE by default: pyannote.audio is NOT installed and is gated behind HF_TOKEN + accepted terms (diarize.py:9-11), DIARIZATION defaults off (config.py:317) — defer that tier. In the default full+multimodal config (MULTIMODAL default "1", config.py:174), download() runs before detection so audio.m4a already exists at detect time; in fast profile there is no audio, so the audio tier must silently no-op.
result.existing_code_audit: detect.py:57 `detect_content_type(transcript, settings)` = ONE Gemini `llm_json` call over title + head+tail sample (config.py:182-184: 25 head segs, 8 tail segs, 3500 chars) — confirms item 22's "samples only head+tail" (middle never sampled). detect.py:14 DetectionResult{content_type,domain,density,confidence,secondary_content_type,rationale} — NO music_fraction/metadata fields yet. detect.py:24-36 CONTENT_TYPE_TO_DOMAIN has NO music/song/comedy/entertainment key → even if the LLM said "comedy" it maps to generic; detect.py:38-43 DETECT_SYSTEM label list has no music/song/comedy/entertainment option (so the LLM can't even emit it). adapters/__init__.py:34 `select_adapter(transcript, settings)` only receives transcript+settings (no audio_path, no yt-dlp meta); registry register/get_adapter at :26-31; 11 adapters registered :45-55 (generic first = fallback). Adding an adapter = one ~40-line class (see story.py) subclassing BaseAdapter with domain/content_types/_domain_role_specs()/_contracts()/labeling_hints(), then import+register() in __init__.py + a CONTENT_TYPE_TO_DOMAIN entry. base.py:145 CORE_VERDICT_FIELDS = the "core verdict fields only" for a relaxed contract; base.py:144 _PROBLEM_ROLES triggers the worked-problem gates (problem_statement/reasoning/result_complete) that killed the parody; base.py:187-200 anchors derive from RoleSpec.anchor_priority. Detection stored on Structure.detection (understand/models.py:156). ORCHESTRATION: orchestrator.py _run_full downloads (when want_mm, ~176) BEFORE select_adapter (:185) — so audio_path exists at detect time in default config but is NOT passed. download.py:142-148 RETURNS only {video_id,video_path,audio_path,title,duration} — it DISCARDS the yt-dlp `info` dict, so categories/tags/artist/track/genre are extracted but thrown away (surface them = a few added dict keys). Gemini multimodal already wired: gemini_client.py:111 generate_json_mm(system, parts, schema) + :101 image_part via types.Part.from_bytes(data, mime_type) — only need an audio_part helper. llm.py:67 llm_json supports model= override; judge already uses it with config.JUDGE_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite (config.py:299, validate.py:150-153). RECONCILIATION integration points: contracts.py:37 choose_contract already picks the contract by CONTENT (unit roles) not anchor; validate.py:133 judge_clip runs per-candidate; both are called in assemble/__init__.py (~246-253 selection, ~392-398 repair, ~550-557) — reconciliation slots right there.
result.recommended_approach: Build the ensemble in detect.py as a WEIGHTED OVERRIDE (not a hard replace), precedence metadata > audio > transcript-stats > LLM, and add an entertainment adapter + a per-candidate reconciliation pass. Concretely: (1) Surface yt-dlp metadata: have download() return categories/tags/artist/track/genre, and add a cheap `extract_info(download=False)` metadata probe so the metadata tier also works in fast/supadata mode; thread an optional `audio_path` + `meta` dict into select_adapter()/detect_content_type(). (2) Metadata tier rules grounded in the real dump: categories∩{Music}→music; categories∩{Comedy,Entertainment} OR title/tags matching music terms (rhapsody|song|parody|lyrics|feat\.|official (music )?video|cover|remix)→entertainment lean; (artist AND track non-null)→music — but treat null artist/track as no-signal (parody case). (3) Audio tier = Gemini-audio, NOT inaSpeechSegmenter: add audio_part() helper (mirror image_part), ffmpeg-cut a ~30-45s MIDDLE window from audio.m4a to mp3, one flash-lite generate_json_mm call returning {is_music, is_sung, speech_vs_music} → music_fraction proxy; only runs when audio_path present, else no-op. (4) Transcript-stats tier: extend _sample to head+MIDDLE+tail; compute line/n-gram repetition + rhyme + short-line ratio → song score (pure Python, no deps; a Bohemian-Rhapsody parody has heavy chorus repetition). (5) Expand DETECT_SYSTEM label set to include music/song/comedy/parody/entertainment and add CONTENT_TYPE_TO_DOMAIN → "entertainment". (6) Reconcile tiers into final content_type/domain/confidence: when the LLM says lecture but metadata=Comedy or audio=music or transcript=song, route to the entertainment adapter, drop confidence, and set secondary_content_type. (7) New EntertainmentAdapter: domain "entertainment", single anchor role "moment" (hook/punchline/quotable) with a minimal contract using CORE_VERDICT_FIELDS ONLY (no _PROBLEM_ROLES gates) — "when unsure, gate less". (8) Reconciliation pass (item 23): per-candidate flash-lite call (reuse llm_json(model=config.JUDGE_MODEL)) beside choose_contract/judge_clip in assemble/__init__.py — cross-check global genre vs the span's ordered unit roles; on contradiction override contract_role to the entertainment "moment" contract so the worked-problem gates don't fire; run it only when genre confidence is low or tiers conflict to bound cost. Fastest path to un-break the 0-clip parody: T2 (entertainment adapter) + minimal metadata routing (categories=Comedy → not lecture) — that alone stops the worked-problem gates from killing every candidate.

open_questions: Do supadata captions carry any speaker labels? If not, item 22's diarization-based interview-vs-story tier is moot by default (pyannote not installed, gated) — confirm before investing.
Acceptable latency/cost of a metadata-only extract_info(download=False) in fast/supadata mode (~1-2s network), or should it be cached per video_id like title.txt?
Should the reconciliation pass run on every candidate, or only when genre confidence < threshold OR tiers conflict, to bound the extra flash-lite calls (12-cand video = 12 calls)?
Entertainment 'moment' clip sizing: still honor the 240s cap + min-duration, and what span budget for a hook/punchline vs a worked example?

Should music_fraction thresholds match the audit's proposal (>0.5 → force music; 0.2-0.5 → secondary+cap confidence at 0.5), or be recalibrated for the Gemini-audio classifier's output rather than inaSpeech's music-time fraction?

When metadata=Comedy but audio=speech (a stand-up lecture vs a sung parody), which tier wins? The precedence says metadata>audio, but the parody needs audio to override a 'Comedy' that could also be spoken.

result.existing_code_audit:
PAYLOAD (backend/orchestrator.py:45-70, `_build_embed_clips`): whitelists n, video_id, facet, reason, start, end, duration, embed_url, path, role, title, context_card, sequence_index, prerequisite_clips, notes. So context_card, prerequisite_clips, notes (the P4b "continues clip N" watch-first cue) and role/title are ALREADY in the payload (orchestrator.py:62-68). DROPPED though present on the spec dict: final_quality (set at assemble/__init__.py:508,529; scoring.py:47), warnings (tuple of short strings, set at boundary_adapt.py:49/93/103, refine.py:134, __init__.py:526-527/545/575 — values: trimmed_start, capped_max_duration, extended_for_min_duration, merged_overlap, merged_severed_pair, missing_context_card, unjudged, unverified_judge_concerns, single_idea_unverified, kill_confirm_unavailable), and ship_flagged (bool asymmetric-gate flag, __init__.py:308/313, boundary_adapt.py:50, integrity.py:78). IMPORTANT: there is NO literal `verdict` key in the final spec dict — the JudgeVerdict lives on cand.verdict (a Candidate attr), not carried into the spec; what survives are the derived signals ship_flagged / hard_gates_ok / n_failure_reasons / n_verified / warnings. So the doc's "verdict" == these signals, not a nested object; do not try to whitelist a nonexistent verdict.
BLEED (backend/orchestrator.py:38-42, `_embed_url`): `?start={int(floor(start))}&end={int(ceil(end)) + 1}&rel=0` — the `+ 1` adds a whole extra second past ceil(end), so e.g. end=42.3 → ceil 43 → 44, ~1.7s bleed into the next sentence. A proven-correct reference already exists: backend/eval/sample_for_labeling.py:59-64 uses `s=max(0,floor(start)); e=max(s+1, ceil(end))` (no +1, guarded), asserted by backend/eval/tests/test_sample_for_labeling.py:62 (end=45.2 → 46). No test pins the orchestrator +1 behavior, so fixing it breaks nothing.
FRONTEND: frontend/src/types.ts:1-17 Clip already declares role?, title?, context_card?, sequence_index?, prerequisite_clips? — but NOT notes, final_quality, warnings, ship_flagged. frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx:29-61 renders ONLY the facet tag, start-end · duration, reason, and export/download button — it renders none of title, context_card, notes, prerequisite_clips, or any quality/warning signal. So context-card + watch-first data is in the payload today and dropped by the UI. ResultsGrid.tsx:4-12 just maps ClipCard; App.tsx:86 passes snap.clips — no change needed there. Reusable Tailwind classes already in ClipCard: `tag` (chip), `glass`, `btn-ghost` (ClipCard.tsx:30/43/50); a components/ui/ dir exists. prerequisite_clips values are sequence indices equal to clip.n (orchestrator.py:52/65, sequence.py), so they map cleanly to a "watch clip N first" link. Test to keep green: backend/eval/tests/test_wave2_columns.py:203 (whitelists_notes) — additive fields won't break it.
result.recommended_approach: Minimal delta = 2 backend edits + 2 frontend edits + a rebuild. BACKEND: (1) In `_build_embed_clips` (orchestrator.py:51-69) add three keys to the emitted dict: `"final_quality": round(float(c.get("final_quality", c.get("score", 0.0))), 3)`, `"warnings": list(c.get("warnings") or ())`, `"ship_flagged": bool(c.get("ship_flagged", False))`. These are the doc's "verdict/warnings/final_quality" (no nested verdict object exists). (2) Fix `_embed_url` (orchestrator.py:38-42): replace `int(math.ceil(end)) + 1` with the guarded no-+1 form mirroring sample_for_labeling.embed_url — `s = max(0, int(math.floor(start))); e = max(s + 1, int(math.ceil(end)))`. Cleanest: import and reuse backend/eval/sample_for_labeling.embed_url (or lift it to a shared util) so the two don't drift; add a small unit test mirroring test_sample_for_labeling.py:62. FRONTEND: (3) types.ts: extend Clip with `notes?: string[]; final_quality?: number; warnings?: string[]; ship_flagged?: boolean;`. (4) ClipCard.tsx: above `reason`, render `title` as a heading when present and `context_card` as a muted intro block when present; render watch-first chip(s) from notes ("continues clip N") and/or prerequisite_clips ("Watch clip N first"); render a quality badge derived from final_quality (e.g. High ≥0.7 / OK otherwise) plus a "Flagged"/warning chip when ship_flagged is true or warnings is non-empty, mapping only a curated user-facing subset of warning strings to short labels and hiding internal ones. Reuse `tag`/`glass`/`btn-ghost`. (5) `cd frontend && npm run build` (optionally `npm run typecheck` first) to regenerate backend/static — REQUIRED or the served app won't change. This is a surfacing/whitelist change only; no pipeline or scoring logic is touched.

result.existing_code_audit: Delta baseline: (1) generate_context_card(spec, units_by_id, adapter, topic) context_card.py:43 builds a <=40-word (config.py:304) grounded 'earlier in the video' preface from spec['referential'] units NOT in the clip + the anchor; returns '' when no referential units lie outside the clip (:48-49); per-sentence rapidfuzz grounding >=55 (:34-40); extractive fallback verbatim summary (:64-69). Reads a dict via .get('unit_ids'/'referential'/'anchor_id'). (2) Card is generated ONLY at STEP 6 (__init__.py:522-531) — AFTER the accept/reject gate, AFTER quality floor (:501) and AFTER max_clips truncation (:517); only survivors get a card. Also in the severed-merge branch (:581). (3) judge_clip ALREADY accepts context_card and injects it in the USER prompt (validate.py:133-134, :143). BUT JUDGE_SYSTEM (validate.py:74-116) never references the card and says 'You see ONLY the clip's transcript ... never the surrounding video' (:75-76) — so a passed card is contradictory dead-weight. (4) THE GATE JUDGES CLIP-ONLY: _judge calls judge_clip with NO card (:724-726, defaults ''); every re-judge seam hardcodes context_card='' (post-snap :255, refund-trim :400, severed-merge :559). (5) MISALIGNMENT: eval comprehension (metrics.py:44-45) judges WITH s['context_card'] while the gate judges WITHOUT. (6) TWO judge KILL gates, both verified+confirmed (unverified_kill=0 invariant): repair terminal validate.py:850-888 (:863-878) and post-snap/post-merge __init__.py:268-288 (:269-276/:277-288). UAM dies when a prereq/reference breaks the hard core (_hard_core_ok validate.py:555 = topic+purpose+grounded+all_references_resolved) verified+confirmed. (7) referential (card source pool) populated by closure with far prereqs it couldn't inline (closure.py:133,:162) — the missing prereq is usually already there. (8) A rescue-set card on the Candidate rides into the spec automatically: types.py:49 Candidate.context_card; boundary_adapt.py:32 copies it (plus judged_text_hash/hard_gates_ok) through snap. (9) Missing-card penalty: _card_warning (__init__.py:40-44) adds 'missing_context_card' -> boundary_score -0.10 (scoring.py:39). (10) NO suppression rule and NO card-as-repair exist (grep confirmed). (11) expand_candidate already maps missing_concept -> introducer unit (validate.py:390-395,:442-445) — reusable to seed the rescue card.
result.recommended_approach: Deliver card-as-repair as a pure ACCEPT-SIDE rescue at the two judge kill gates plus one load-bearing prompt fix. (A) PROMPT FIX (prerequisite for everything; also fixes eval): amend JUDGE_SYSTEM (validate.py:74-116) with 'If a CONTEXT CARD is provided it is shown to the viewer immediately before the clip; treat any concept it introduces or reference it resolves as available to the viewer — such concepts satisfy prerequisites_satisfied and such references satisfy all_references_resolved.' Without this the injected card (validate.py:143) is ignored. (B) CARD-AS-REPAIR at the repair terminal gate: in validate_and_repair, after 'confirmed' is computed (validate.py:863-867) and BEFORE the 'if confirmed:' kill (:868): if confirmed and every confirmed reason is prereq/reference-family, generate a card from ship.referential (dict shim over generate_context_card) and RE-JUDGE THE SAME SPAN (ship.i_start..ship.i_end, no growth) WITH the card; if the carded verdict clears the hard core AND prerequisites_satisfied AND all_references_resolved, set ship.context_card + 'card_completed' warning, return _stamp_trims(ship),None; else fall through to the existing kill. (C) CARD-AS-REPAIR at the post-snap/post-merge gate: mirror (B) in __init__.py after confirmed (:274-276) before the Rejection (:278) — spec is a dict so generate_context_card(s,...) works directly; thread s.get('context_card','') into the post-snap judge_clip (:253-255) and refund-trim (:398-400) so a card-completed clip keeps its card when re-judged after a text change. (D) SUPPRESSION + no-clobber at step 6 (__init__.py:522-531): skip generation when the first sentence already names the subject (grounded fuzzy match of topic/anchor primary concept vs sentences[start].text); change :525 to 'if not s.get(\"context_card\"): ...' so a rescue-set card survives. INVARIANT: every insert is accept-side-only — each converts a would-be 'return None, Rejection' into 'return ship/spec, None' and never creates a new Rejection; the verified+confirmed kill logic is untouched, so unverified_kill stays structurally 0. Robustness (T5): also seed the card from the verdict's missing_concept -> introducers -> prior_introducer (reuse validate.py:390-395) when the prereq isn't in referential; keep the card grounded via context_card._grounded so it can never fabricate the missing concept — no source unit means no card and the kill correctly stands.

proposed_tasks.notes: Insert between confirmed-computed (:867) and the kill (:868). _card_rescuable(confirmed)=all reasons prereq/reference-family (reuse _norm_kind). _try_card_rescue: generate_context_card({'unit_ids':ship.unit_ids,'referential':ship.referential,'anchor_id':ship.anchor_id},...) then judge_clip(same-span text, context_card=card); accept iff _hard_core_ok AND prerequisites_satisfied AND all_references_resolved. Success: set ship.context_card + 'card_completed', return _stamp_trims(ship),None. All deps in scope. Accept-side only -> invariant safe.

proposed_tasks.title: Card-as-repair at post-snap/post-merge gate + thread existing card into re-judge seams
proposed_tasks.notes: Mirror T2 after confirmed at :276 before the Rejection at :278 (spec dict -> generate_context_card(s,...) directly). Change context_card='' to s.get('context_card','') at :255 and :400 so a rescued clip keeps its card across a text-change re-judge. Severed-merge (:559) is a KEEP-linked-pair path (no kill) — pass the card for consistency only. Accept-side only.

proposed_tasks.title: Suppression rule + don't-clobber-rescued-card at step 6
proposed_tasks.notes: Change :525 to 'if not s.get("context_card"): s["context_card"]=generate_context_card(...)' so rescue cards survive. Suppress when a normalized fuzzy match of topic/anchor primary concept appears in sentences[sentence_start_idx].text. Risk is definitional ('the subject') plus SciTalk card-redundancy stop-signal — keep suppression conservative and grounded.

proposed_tasks.title: Robust rescue seeding from the verdict's missing_concept introducer
proposed_tasks.notes: When the prereq's introducer isn't in referential, map missing_concept -> introducers -> prior_introducer (reuse validate.py:390-395) and add that unit to the card's allowed set, still grounded via _grounded. Makes the UAM rescue robust to closure not queueing the prereq. Grounding prevents fabrication.

proposed_tasks.title: Scoring/warnings for card_completed and suppressed cards
proposed_tasks.notes: Ensure a card_completed clip isn't also tagged missing_context_card, and a deliberately SUPPRESSED card doesn't trip the -0.10 penalty (suppression is intentional). Optionally give card_completed a small completeness/boundary credit so a rescued clip clears the quality floor it was already near.

proposed_tasks.title: Tests: invariant regression + UAM-shape rescue fixture
proposed_tasks.notes: Assert unverified_kill stays 0 across the new paths (a would-be kill with an empty/non-verifiable card must NOT kill and must NOT rescue). UAM fixture: hard core broken by a prereq whose introducer is in referential; card flips prerequisites_satisfied/all_references_resolved -> ship carded SAME span (i_start/i_end and unit_ids unchanged = 'without growing'). Negative: confirmed missing_result (not rescuable) still dies.

open_questions: Truly always-on card even when referential is empty: item 20/ReelsEd wants a one-line orientation LABEL on every clip, but today's card requires referential context (returns ''). Build a separate cheap label (extractive from anchor title/topic, possibly no LLM) vs keep referential-sourced only? Risk: SciTalk acceptance criterion 4 flags card redundancy as a stop signal.
Suppression predicate: what is 'the subject' — query topic, anchor primary concept, or clip title head noun — and what fuzzy threshold? Needs a grounded, tested rule to avoid over/under-suppression.

Should the rescue re-judge count against JUDGE_MAX_REPAIR (config.py:284=3) budget, or be a free post-budget kill-path call like confirm_kill? Recommend free/post-budget, bounded to 1 card + 1 judge, since it runs only when the clip would otherwise die.

T1 changes the judge prompt: per acceptance criterion 3 gate on judge-human kappa >= 0.4 (no regression). It also moves eval comprehension upward (eval already injects the card at metrics.py:45) — is that shift an acceptable real alignment gain, and does the labeling set need re-scoring?

Does judging clip+card on the ACCEPT side of EVERY repair pass (fuller alignment) beat rescue-only-at-the-kill-boundary? Rescue-at-boundary alone removes the harmful asymmetry (eval-passes-but-gate-kills); per-pass carding adds cost + card staleness across grow/trim. Recommend deferring per-pass carding.

result.area: Wave 3 items 18-19 — 2-of-3 boundary gate {restored terminator, SaT ±1 word, inter-word pause ≥0.25s} + cut-inside-pause; and precise cuts by forced alignment (torchaudio.functional.forced_align / MFA) vs the current WhisperX-style pass. Verdict: forced alignment is LOW ROI and effectively blocked on this box; the residual boundary error is segmentation (wrong word), not timing (wrong millisecond).
result.current_tool_state: torchaudio.functional.forced_align: signature F.forced_align(emission, targets, blank=0); emissions come from torchaudio.pipelines.MMS_FA (a wav2vec2 CTC model), CPU-supported. STATUS CONTESTED in 2026: deprecated in 2.8, slated for removal in 2.9 (matches the doc's "pin <2.9"); the CTC forced-alignment tutorial (docs.pytorch.org/audio/stable) now states it was "removed in 2.9, migrate to TorchCodec," while another 2026 source reports forced_align was PRESERVED in 2.10 after community feedback. torchaudio latest = 2.11.0 (macOS arm64 wheels incl. cp312). Decisive fact independent of the API debate: torchaudio must match torch exactly, and this venv has torch 2.12.1 (ahead of torchaudio 2.11.0) — no matching torchaudio wheel exists. wtpsplit / wtpsplit-lite (SaT, EMNLP 2024): current, pip-installable; models sat-3l / sat-3l-sm (fast) and sat-12l / sat-12l-sm (best); wtpsplit-lite 0.2.0 is ONNX-only with minimal deps and runs on CPU by default (onnxruntime 1.27.0 already in venv). NOT installed. Montreal-Forced-Aligner 3.3.8: distributed via conda-forge (bundles Kaldi/pynini/openfst); pip-from-github possible but still needs the conda Kaldi stack; conda is NOT on this box. parakeet-mlx (item 17's "better long-term"): current on PyPI, Apple-Silicon-native (MLX), model mlx-community/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3, emits word timestamps + punctuation + capitalization, ~6% WER; installable via pip/pipx. faster-whisper 1.2.1 + ctranslate2 4.8.0 ARE installed (the engine the current boundary.py WhisperX-style pass uses). google-genai 2.10.0 installed; Context7 confirms the CURRENT API is client.models.generate_content(model=..., config=types.GenerateContentConfig(response_mime_type='application/json', response_schema=PydanticModel)) — exactly what backend/llm.py uses; a newer response_json_schema field and gemini-3.5-flash now exist, but the repo's gemini-2.5-flash (author) / gemini-2.5-flash-lite (judge) remain valid. pysbd 0.3.4 current.
result.environment_feasibility: darwin arm64, Python 3.12.13, clips/.venv. (1) torchaudio forced_align (item 19): EFFECTIVELY BLOCKED. torchaudio's latest 2.11.0 pairs with torch 2.11, but this venv runs torch 2.12.1; installing torchaudio would force-downgrade torch, and torch 2.12.1 is load-bearing — it is pulled in by sentence-transformers 3.4.1 (used by backend/pipeline/understand/treeseg.py embeddings) and transformers/safetensors, i.e. the TreeSeg structure stage. Downgrading torch to satisfy torchaudio risks breaking the structure stage. Plus the forced_align API itself is on a removal/TorchCodec-migration path. Not worth it. (2) MFA (item 19 alt): conda not installed; requires a fresh miniconda + a ~GB Kaldi/pynini/openfst env; CLI-subprocess model; high friction, off-stack. Low feasibility. (3) wtpsplit-lite SaT (items 17/18 signal 2): FEASIBLE now — ONNX on CPU, onnxruntime 1.27.0 already present, one model download (~few hundred MB for sat-3l). (4) parakeet-mlx (item 17 long-term): FEASIBLE on this exact hardware (Apple Silicon MLX), but needs the audio downloaded (yt-dlp) + a model download, and re-transcribes the whole video. (5) The existing faster-whisper boundary window: deps already present (faster-whisper 1.2.1, ffmpeg at /opt/homebrew/bin/ffmpeg); its only gate is a successful yt-dlp audio download (residential-IP-dependent per the hosting-vidscout memory).

THE WHISPERX PASS item 19 wants to replace = backend/pipeline/boundary.py:150 refine_clip_boundaries: _ensure_audio (yt-dlp, boundary.py:28) → _whisper_window (±BOUNDARY_PAD_S=10s, boundary.py:70) → build_sentence_index on a FRESH faster-whisper transcription → _pick_start/_pick_end. It IS wired (orchestrator.py:225 structure, :260 fast) but gated on config.PRECISE_BOUNDARIES (default on, config.py:62) AND source=='supadata'; returns clips unchanged on audio failure (boundary.py:158). DESIGN SMELL: it re-segments from Whisper-small's OWN punctuation, discarding the LLM-restored terminators, so it can move a good boundary to a worse Whisper one — and although the window carries REAL word times, it uses them for nothing pause-related. PAUSE SIGNAL REALITY (item 18 signal 3): chunker._pause_gaps exists (punctuation/chunker.py:18) and is used with 0.7s thresholds, but for supadata word times are SYNTHESIZED proportionally within each caption chunk (transcribe.py:163-171). I proved empirically that intra-chunk inter-word gaps are all exactly 0.0; the only nonzero gap sits at the ~180-char caption-chunk boundary (SUPADATA_CHUNK_SIZE=180, config.py:34). So the pause signal is UNAVAILABLE within a chunk on supadata. tail_pad_s=0.05 fixed (config.py:121); cut.py:74 uses cut_end=end+tail_pad. No SaT/wtpsplit, no forced alignment, no torchaudio anywhere in the repo.
result.recommended_approach: Do the cheap, feasible, high-value subset of item 18 and SKIP item 19's forced alignment. The audit's own evidence (both audits: ends_on_period_rate=1.0 yet clips end mid-sentence — kinematics 'negative 20', trio 'in order') shows the DOMINANT residual boundary error is SEGMENTATION (the boundary is at the wrong word: a fabricated period from sentences_from_chunks, or an LLM period where the speaker didn't stop), not TIMING. Forced alignment only fixes timing (~20ms MFA vs ~110ms WhisperX), so it targets the wrong error, buys an imperceptible ~90ms over the already-installed faster-whisper window, and is blocked here anyway (torch 2.12.1 vs torchaudio 2.11.0; installing risks breaking sentence-transformers/TreeSeg). Concretely: (1) Land the FREE text-only guards from item 18 — reject an end boundary whose final sentence has <3 words or ends on a conjunction/preposition; kill sentences_from_chunks' fabricated terminators. (2) Add SaT (wtpsplit-lite, CPU) on the fallback/run-on paths (item 17) and use it as gate signal 2 (SaT boundary ±1 word) — this directly attacks the segmentation error. (3) Implement the 2-of-3 gate as {LLM terminator (free), SaT ±1 word, pause≥0.25s}, but be honest that on supadata the pause signal only exists at caption-chunk boundaries — it becomes a real signal ONLY when real word times are available. (4) For real word times + cut-inside-pause, REPURPOSE the existing faster-whisper window: instead of re-segmenting via Whisper's punctuation, keep the LLM-chosen boundary word/text and use the window only to (a) re-TIME that exact boundary and (b) measure the following pause, then place the physical cut inside it (replacing fixed tail_pad_s). That is 'forced-alignment semantics' with a tool already installed, and it removes the current regression risk of overriding LLM boundaries. If, after (1)-(4), boundary PRECISION is still the bottleneck (verify against a golden set — none exists yet), the right lever is parakeet-mlx (item 17 long-term) to replace supadata's synthesized timestamps at the root, NOT torchaudio forced alignment. Everything audio-based (pause, re-timing, parakeet) is contingent on yt-dlp audio download actually succeeding on this box — confirm that first, else only the text-only signals (1)-(2) apply.

result.current_tool_state: parakeet-mlx is real, current, and Apple-Silicon-native. PyPI latest = 0.5.2 (released 2026-06-05), license Apache-2.0, requires_python >=3.10 (so 3.12 works). Install deps: dacite>=1.9.2, huggingface-hub>=0.30.2, librosa>=0.11.0, mlx>=0.22.1, numpy>=2.2.5, typer>=0.15.3 — NO torch (pure MLX). `pip install parakeet-mlx -U`; needs system ffmpeg (already have config.FFMPEG_BIN). Python API (verified from GitHub README): `from parakeet_mlx import from_pretrained; model = from_pretrained("mlx-community/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3"); result = model.transcribe("audio.wav")`. Result is `AlignedResult` with `.text` and `.sentences`; each `AlignedSentence` has text/start/end/duration/tokens; each `AlignedToken` (word-level) has text/start/end/duration. So it emits BOTH real word-level AND sentence-level timestamps natively, plus punctuation + capitalization — exactly the two things the supadata path fakes. Long audio: `model.transcribe(path, chunk_duration=120.0, overlap_duration=15.0)` handles >24min. Model mlx-community/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3 = 2.51 GB download to HF cache, weights CC-BY-4.0 (NVIDIA parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3, 600M params, 25 European languages, auto language-detect, ~6.34% WER per design doc). Min 2GB unified memory. RTF ≈ 0.042 on M4 (~24x realtime, ~53-68s per hour of audio); M1/M2/M3 not officially benchmarked (will be slower but still far above faster-whisper-small-int8-on-CPU).
result.environment_feasibility: Installable here with no hard blockers. Host = darwin arm64, macOS 26.5, Python 3.12.13, clips/.venv (confirmed via .venv/bin/python). mlx ships prebuilt arm64 wheels (macOS >=13.5, Apple Silicon only) — no native build, no CUDA, no compiler needed; this Mac qualifies. numpy 2.2.6 is already installed and satisfies parakeet's >=2.2.5. parakeet-mlx has NO torch dependency, so the repo's torch 2.12.1 / ctranslate2 / faster-whisper 1.2.1 stack is untouched (no conflict). Only genuinely new transitive weight: librosa (pulls numba/llvmlite/soundfile) — all have arm64 wheels, installs clean, ~tens of MB. Costs: one-time 2.51 GB model download + ~2.5 GB disk; no per-video API cost (removes supadata quota use on the ASR path). mlx is currently NOT installed (verified: `import mlx` fails). Audio input needs 16 kHz mono, which download.py already produces (TARGET_AUDIO_SR=16000, TARGET_AUDIO_CH=1). Net: feasible, medium install footprint, no licensing blocker (Apache-2.0 code, CC-BY-4.0 weights → just attribution).
result.existing_code_audit: Two transcribe paths in backend/pipeline/transcribe.py. (1) transcribe_supadata() (transcribe.py:136-184) is the DEFAULT main flow: it fetches supadata captions and SYNTHESIZES per-word timestamps by evenly spacing tokens inside each ~180-char caption chunk (transcribe.py:166-171) — these fake gaps are the core weakness item 17 targets; it tags source="supadata" (transcribe.py:178) and keeps raw chunks. (2) transcribe() (transcribe.py:187-219) is the local faster_whisper / Groq path, gated on config.TRANSCRIBER; _get_whisper() (transcribe.py:25-32) lazy-loads faster-whisper (device=cpu, int8, model small). config.TRANSCRIBER defaults to "supadata" (config.py:26); options documented as supadata|faster_whisper|groq. Orchestrator branches at orchestrator.py:86-103 (supadata→transcribe_supadata, else download+transcribe); cli.py:46/50 mirrors. So the local-ASR path already EXISTS and is wired — adding "parakeet" is a third branch, not new plumbing. Punctuation: the LLM restoration pipeline (backend/pipeline/punctuation/, entry service.py) is gated by build_sentences() (service.py:256-287): it runs LLM restoration only when config.PUNCTUATION_ENABLED and _needs_punctuation(transcript) is true; _needs_punctuation (service.py:242-253) forces restoration for source=="supadata" (line 245) OR when terminal-punct/word ratio < 0.02 (line 253) — a terminal-ratio auto-trigger concept ALREADY exists, just keyed to "should I punctuate", not "should I ASR". Because restoration keys on source=="supadata", a parakeet transcript (source!="supadata", high terminal ratio) would automatically SKIP LLM restoration and take the legacy/native sentence path (service.py:281-287) — so parakeet natively displaces the Gemini punctuation round-trip and its degraded-cache failure modes (documented in service.py:96-141, 225-232). Also note boundary.py (item 19 territory) ALREADY re-runs faster-whisper on small windows for real word timestamps at cut boundaries (boundary.py:23,80-81) — so precise CUT timing is already recovered locally; the gap parakeet fills is real word timing across the WHOLE transcript for pause detection during selection/gating. Design doc item 17 lives at docs/CLIP-QUALITY-2026-07-02-audit-and-fix-plan.md:163-168.
result.recommended_approach: ADOPT, but gated + sequenced — do NOT make it the default transcriber. Add a third TRANSCRIBER value "parakeet" plus a cheap-probe auto-trigger, and land it alongside item 18 (the 2-of-3 boundary gate) so the real word timestamps it produces are actually consumed. Rationale for adopt (not redundant): the LLM punctuation package gives clean SENTENCES, but it runs on SYNTHESIZED word timings (transcribe.py:166-171), so item 18's inter-word-pause signal (≥0.25s) and PUNCT_PAUSE_* thresholds are operating on fake gaps for every supadata video — parakeet is the only thing that makes that pause signal real. Parakeet also gives native punctuation, so it removes the per-video Gemini restoration call and its degraded-cache pathology for exactly the worst inputs. Rationale for gating (not default): supadata's killer feature is zero audio download (orchestrator.py:88-93) — a big latency win for well-captioned videos, which the LLM restoration already handles fine. So implement the design's auto-trigger as a PROBE-FIRST fallback: (a) fetch supadata captions (cheap), (b) compute the fraction of caption chunks ending in terminal punctuation, (c) if <20% (the doc threshold; today service.py:283-285 uses a sibling frac<0.3 chunk-fallback), download audio and re-transcribe with parakeet, yielding source="parakeet" so the punctuation seam auto-skips LLM restoration and the pipeline consumes true word timings. This targets the exact low-quality regime where LLM restoration works hardest on the fakest timing, while leaving the fast path intact. Defer flipping the default to parakeet until item 18/19 ship and a golden-set A/B shows a boundary-quality win (golden set is still pending per memory). Net: medium effort, low-med risk (isolated new branch, supadata stays default, deps install clean), med-high value concentrated on bad-caption videos.

plan.executive_summary:
Most of the "new" work is smaller than the audit implies — the plumbing already exists; the plan is a DELTA, not a rewrite.

WHAT ALREADY EXISTS (verified): (1) Frontend — context_card/role/title/prerequisite_clips/notes are ALREADY in the embed payload (orchestrator.py:62-68) and ALREADY declared on the Clip type; they are simply dropped by ClipCard.tsx. Surfacing = whitelist 3 more keys + render + rebuild. (2) Card-as-repair — reuses two SHIPPING functions, generate_context_card (context_card.py:43) and judge_clip (validate.py:133, which already injects a card into the user prompt); the only missing pieces are a JUDGE_SYSTEM line crediting the card and two accept-side rescue inserts at the existing kill gates. (3) Genre — the adapter registry, single-LLM detection, and the supadata/whisper/local transcriber branch all exist; download() already fetches the yt-dlp info dict and then THROWS AWAY categories/tags (download.py:142-148). Un-breaking the 0-clip parody = surface metadata + one ~40-line EntertainmentAdapter (CORE_VERDICT_FIELDS only, avoiding the _PROBLEM_ROLES gates that killed it) + a detect.py label/route entry. (4) Video judge is genuinely greenfield but purely additive: google-genai 2.10.0 is installed with full VideoMetadata/Files-API support; it extends the existing generate_json_mm pattern and must be Gemini-only (Groq has no video) and advisory-only.

THE BIG RE-SCOPING CALLS: (a) DROP torchaudio forced_align — blocked here (torch 2.12.1 has no matching torchaudio wheel; installing downgrades torch and risks breaking sentence-transformers 3.4.1/TreeSeg) AND it fixes the wrong error: both audits show ends_on_period_rate=1.0 yet clips end mid-clause, i.e. the dominant residual error is SEGMENTATION (wrong word), not ~90ms TIMING. Fix segmentation with FREE text-only guards. (b) DROP inaSpeechSegmenter — unpinned TensorFlow+Pyro4 into the shared torch/numpy venv, Metal dead on py3.12; a one-call Gemini-audio classify gives equivalent music-vs-speech discrimination at zero venv risk. (c) DEFER SaT/wtpsplit — largely REDUNDANT with the LLM punctuation restoration that already produces terminators for the default supadata source (service.py:245,264-277); it only helps the rare sentences_from_chunks fallback, whose highest-value fix (killing fabricated terminators) is free. (d) DEFER parakeet default-flip — adopt opt-in/auto-trigger only; the pause signal it would feed is currently fake on supadata (proven intra-chunk gaps=0.0) and there is no golden set to A/B a default flip.

INVARIANT: every quality change is invariant-safe by construction — card-as-repair only converts a would-be Rejection into a ship (never creates a Rejection; empty/non-verifiable card must NOT rescue), video/edge gates are warning+dock only, and the EntertainmentAdapter gates LESS. unverified_kill stays structurally 0. This must be pinned by a regression test (CARD4).

sequenced_tasks.rationale: judge_clip already injects the card (validate.py:143) but JUDGE_SYSTEM says 'you see ONLY the transcript' (validate.py:75) so the card is contradictory dead-weight. Tell the judge a provided card is shown-before-the-clip and satisfies prerequisites_satisfied/all_references_resolved. Prerequisite for ALL card-as-repair. Prompt change → gate on judge-human kappa>=0.4 and run --freeze --runs 3 A/B; eval comprehension will shift up (eval already injects the card at metrics.py:45), which is a real alignment gain, not noise.

sequenced_tasks.title: Card-as-repair rescue at the repair terminal kill gate (THE UAM/equations-clip fix)
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Insert between confirmed-computed (validate.py:867) and the kill (validate.py:868). If every confirmed reason is prereq/reference-family, generate a card from ship.referential and RE-JUDGE the SAME span with the card; accept iff hard-core + prerequisites_satisfied + all_references_resolved, set 'card_completed' warning, return _stamp_trims(ship),None; else fall through to existing kill. Accept-side only → converts a would-be Rejection to a ship, never creates one. +1 card +1 judge on the rare kill path only (like confirm_kill). The single most important quality item.

sequenced_tasks.title: Card-as-repair at post-snap/post-merge gate + thread existing card into re-judge seams
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Mirror CARD2 after confirmed (__init__.py:276) before the Rejection (:278) — spec is a dict so generate_context_card(s,...) works directly. Also change context_card='' to s.get('context_card','') at the post-snap judge (:255) and refund-trim (:400) re-judge seams so a rescued clip keeps its card across text-change re-judges. Accept-side only.

sequenced_tasks.title: Invariant regression test + UAM rescue fixture + card_completed scoring/warnings
sequenced_tasks.rationale: HARD INVARIANT gate: assert unverified_kill stays 0 across the new paths — a would-be kill with an empty/non-verifiable card must NOT kill AND must NOT rescue (negative: confirmed missing_result still dies). UAM fixture: hard core broken by a prereq whose introducer is in referential; card flips prerequisites_satisfied/all_references_resolved and ships the SAME span (i_start/i_end/unit_ids unchanged). Also ensure a card_completed clip isn't double-tagged missing_context_card (no spurious -0.10 dock). Must ship WITH CARD2/CARD3.

sequenced_tasks.title: Cheap text-only boundary guards + kill fabricated chunk terminators
sequenced_tasks.rationale: sentences_from_chunks (sentences.py:376-399) fabricates ends_with_period=True for every caption chunk — the boundary-neutralizer that makes snapping a no-op (audit: ends_on_period_rate=1.0 yet mid-clause ends). Mark chunk-boundary ends as weak; reject an end whose final sentence has <3 words or ends on a conjunction/preposition. FREE (no deps), attacks the DOMINANT segmentation error, moves boundaries (never kills a clip) → invariant-safe.

sequenced_tasks.title: Genre ensemble core: metadata tier + transcript-stats (repetition/rhyme) + head+middle+tail sampling + tier reconcile
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Weighted override (metadata>audio>transcript-stats>LLM), not a hard replace. _sample currently reads head+tail only (detect.py:47-54) — item 22's gap; add MIDDLE. Pure-Python song score (line/n-gram repetition + rhyme + short-line ratio) catches a Bohemian-Rhapsody parody's chorus repetition. Extend DetectionResult with music_fraction/signals. When LLM says lecture but metadata=Comedy/audio=music/transcript=song, route to entertainment, drop confidence, set secondary_content_type.

sequenced_tasks.title: Gemini-audio music-check tier (audio_part helper + detect_audio module) — NOT inaSpeechSegmenter
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Add audio_part() mirroring image_part (Part.from_bytes(mime_type='audio/mpeg'), confirmed current in google-genai 2.10.0), ffmpeg-cut a ~30-45s MIDDLE window from audio.m4a to mp3, one flash-lite generate_json_mm classify -> {is_music,is_sung,speech_vs_music} music_fraction proxy. Zero new deps, Apple-Silicon-native, one LLM call. Only runs when audio_path present (no-op in fast profile). Cache next to work/<id>/. Explicitly rejects the heavy TensorFlow/Pyro4 tree.

sequenced_tasks.title: Robust rescue seeding from verdict missing_concept introducer + suppression/no-clobber at step 6
sequenced_tasks.rationale: When the prereq's introducer isn't in referential, map missing_concept->introducers->prior_introducer (reuse validate.py:390-395) and add that unit to the card's allowed set, still grounded via context_card._grounded so it can never fabricate (no source unit -> no card -> kill correctly stands). Also at step-6 card generation (__init__.py:522-531) change to 'if not s.get("context_card")' so a rescue card survives, and suppress generation when the first sentence already names the subject (conservative grounded fuzzy match). Makes the UAM rescue robust to closure not queueing the prereq.

sequenced_tasks.title: gemini_client.generate_json_video + video Part helpers (inline bytes, Files upload+ACTIVE poll, video_metadata offsets, media_resolution)
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Enabler for both video tiers. Verified against installed SDK: video_part_inline via types.Part.from_bytes(mime_type='video/mp4', media_resolution=LOW); video_part_uri via Part(file_data=FileData(uri,mime), video_metadata=VideoMetadata(start_offset='120s',end_offset='360s',fps=1.0)); upload_video(path)->poll files.get until FileState.ACTIVE. Reuse generate_json_mm's thinking-off-then-on + _retry config. Gemini-only, MUST bypass llm.py (Groq has no video), exactly as vision.py does.

sequenced_tasks.title: Edge-probe: cut 8s head/tail via cut.build_cmd, inline video judge (advisory), orchestrator hook after refine_clip_boundaries
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Cheapest video win (~$0.0001-0.0004/clip, inline <20MB, no Files upload). Catches the F7 mid-sentence-audio blind spot Whisper can't hear. Hook AFTER refine_clip_boundaries (orchestrator.py:227) so audited offsets == shipped offsets. Advisory only: False nudges the snap to the nearest sentence/silence edge or warns starts/ends_mid_sentence_audio — NEVER kills. MEASURE residual after BND1/BND2 before trusting it. Needs source video present (download if absent in embed mode).

sequenced_tasks.title: Config flags + plumb video/edge outcomes into warnings/final_quality/eval/artifacts
sequenced_tasks.rationale: EDGE_PROBE_ENABLED / VIDEO_JUDGE_ENABLED / VIDEO_JUDGE_MODEL (default gemini-2.5-flash-lite) / media_resolution default LOW, all default OFF mirroring DIARIZATION_ENABLED. Add starts_clean_audio/ends_clean_audio/visuals_referenced_are_visible to the shipped record + eval columns; keep them ADVISORY (rates reported, not gating) until per-label kappa exists. Also fixes item 24's metrics.py:44 visual_summary='' by threading the real summary.

sequenced_tasks.title: Repurpose faster-whisper window: re-TIME the LLM-chosen boundary + cut inside the following pause (replaces WhisperX re-segmentation + fixed tail_pad)
sequenced_tasks.rationale: refine_clip_boundaries (boundary.py:150) re-segments from Whisper-small's OWN punctuation, discarding LLM-restored terminators — a design smell that can move a good boundary to a worse one. Instead keep the LLM boundary word and use the ±10s window ONLY to (a) re-time it and (b) measure the following silence, cutting inside the pause (replaces fixed tail_pad_s=0.05). Gets 'forced-alignment semantics' with deps already installed (faster-whisper 1.2.1). Gated on yt-dlp audio succeeding; returns unchanged on failure. Measure vs BND1 before/after on a golden set.

sequenced_tasks.title: Render-audit on survivors (Files upload source once + per-clip offsets, VideoVerdict) — opt-in, default OFF
sequenced_tasks.rationale: One client.files.upload + ACTIVE poll per video, N cheap offset generate_content calls (media_resolution=LOW, fps=1.0). VideoVerdict adds starts_clean_audio/ends_clean_audio/visuals_referenced_are_visible + short evidence. Wire as WARNINGS + a small final_quality dock (mirror the W25-E single_idea advisory at validate.py:308) — MUST NOT override the asymmetric quote-verified text kill gate. ~$0.01-0.30/video, survivors only. Lower priority than edge-probe; measure whether it adds signal.

sequenced_tasks.title: Reconciliation pass: per-candidate genre-vs-role-sequence cross-check + contract/adapter override
sequenced_tasks.rationale: Reuse llm_json(model=JUDGE_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite) beside choose_contract/judge_clip (__init__.py ~246-253/392-398/550-557). Cross-check global genre vs the span's ordered unit roles; on contradiction override contract_role to the entertainment 'moment' contract so worked-problem gates don't fire. Gate on LOW genre confidence / tier conflict to bound cost (12 candidates could = 12 calls). Belt-and-suspenders for the parody once GEN2/GEN3 land; lower ROI than the detection-side fix.

sequenced_tasks.title: parakeet-mlx opt-in transcriber + probe-first auto-trigger + config (defer default flip)
sequenced_tasks.rationale: parakeet-mlx 0.5.2 installs clean on this arm64 box (pure MLX, no torch dep, numpy 2.2.6 satisfies >=2.2.5; only new transitive is librosa; 2.51GB one-time model). Adds a THIRD TRANSCRIBER branch (plumbing exists at transcribe.py:187-219). Probe-first: fetch supadata captions, if <20% chunks end in terminal punct AND language is one of the 25 European langs, download audio + transcribe_parakeet -> source='parakeet' auto-skips the LLM punctuation seam (service.py:245) and yields REAL word timings (the only true fix for supadata's synthesized gaps=0.0). Keep TRANSCRIBER default 'supadata'; DEFER default flip until a golden-set A/B shows a boundary win.

deferred_or_dropped: DROP torchaudio forced_align (w3-boundaries T5): torch 2.12.1 has NO matching torchaudio wheel (latest 2.11.0); installing force-downgrades torch and risks breaking sentence-transformers 3.4.1/TreeSeg (the structure stage). forced_align is also on a deprecation/TorchCodec-removal path. Worst of all it fixes the WRONG error — both audits show ends_on_period_rate=1.0 yet mid-clause ends, so the dominant residual is segmentation (wrong word), not ~90ms timing. BND1+BND2 cover the real error with already-installed deps.

DROP inaSpeechSegmenter (w4-genre T6): pulls unpinned TensorFlow (~0.5-1GB) + deprecated Pyro4 + pyannote.core into the SHARED venv holding torch 2.12/numpy 2.2.6 (high protobuf/numpy resolver-churn risk); tensorflow-metal has no py3.12 wheel so it's CPU-only anyway. GEN4 (one Gemini-audio call) gives equivalent music-vs-speech/singing discrimination at zero venv risk. Never add TF/Pyro4 to clips/.venv.

DEFER SaT/wtpsplit-lite (w3-boundaries T2 + the full 2-of-3 gate T3): largely REDUNDANT with the LLM punctuation restoration that already produces terminators for the default supadata source (service.py:245,264-277). SaT only adds value on the rare sentences_from_chunks fallback, whose highest-value fix (killing fabricated terminators) is free in BND1. The 2-of-3 gate's pause signal is also proven fake on supadata (intra-chunk gaps=0.0). Revisit only if BND1 proves insufficient on run-on captions.

DEFER diarization-based interview-vs-story genre tier (w4-genre item 22): pyannote.audio is NOT installed, gated behind HF_TOKEN + accepted terms, DIARIZATION defaults off; supadata captions likely carry no speaker labels. Low ROI until diarization is enabled.

DEFER parakeet default-flip (w3-asr T17-5): adopt opt-in/auto-trigger (ASR1) only. Do not flip TRANSCRIBER default until a golden set A/B shows a boundary-quality win — the golden set does not exist yet, and auto-trigger forces an audio download that negates supadata's no-download latency edge.

DROP per-pass carding on every repair accept (w3-cards open question): rescue-at-the-kill-boundary (CARD2/CARD3) alone removes the harmful eval-passes-but-gate-kills asymmetry; per-pass carding adds cost + card staleness across grow/trim for no additional rescue.

DEFER render-audit media_resolution MEDIUM/HIGH: keep LOW (~100 tok/s) default; only raise (3x cost) if visuals_referenced_are_visible under-detects small on-screen text/equations on slide-heavy content.
decisions_needed_from_user: Does yt-dlp AUDIO download reliably succeed in production on this Mac (residential-IP dependency per the hosting-vidscout memory)? It GATES BND2, ASR1/parakeet, VID2/VID3 source, and GEN4's audio tier. Evidence says likely yes — PRECISE_BOUNDARIES is default-on and refine_clip_boundaries already needs audio (orchestrator.py:222-225) — but confirm before investing in the audio-based half.

Should any video/edge gate EVER kill, or stay advisory-only until judge-human kappa>=0.4 is established? Recommendation: advisory-only (warnings + small final_quality dock), consistent with the W25-E single_idea pattern — this is what keeps the unverified_kill=0 invariant intact. Confirm.

Genre precedence when metadata=Comedy but audio=speech (a stand-up/spoken 'comedy' lecture) vs a SUNG parody: which tier decides music-vs-entertainment-spoken? Recommendation: the GEN4 audio tier decides is_music/is_sung; metadata only nudges routing AWAY from lecture. Confirm the rule so a spoken-comedy talk isn't mis-forced to a music path.

CARD1 changes the JUDGE_SYSTEM prompt and will move eval comprehension UP (eval already injects the card at metrics.py:45). Approve gating this on judge-human kappa>=0.4 (no regression) and re-scoring the labeling set if needed — this is a real alignment gain, not noise.

Bias-hygiene / product call: show a quality + flag badge on the PRODUCTION clip card (FE3), or keep it subtle/opt-in? The labeling page (sample_for_labeling.py:116-118) deliberately hides judge internals near the embed. Also approve the user-facing warning->label allowlist (e.g. unverified_judge_concerns->'unverified', trimmed_start->'trimmed', capped_max_duration->'length-capped'; hide merged_overlap/single_idea_unverified/missing_context_card/unjudged).

Build a small golden set now (boundary-labeled + genre-labeled) — it is a PREREQUISITE to objectively justify BND2, VID2 Tier-2, and any parakeet default flip (eval_baseline boundary_error is currently NaN; no eval/golden/*.json). You already owe ~30 clip labels per memory; extend that pass to cover boundary + genre.

Video-judge cost/rate-limit posture: ship both tiers default OFF opt-in (mirror DIARIZATION_ENABLED) and pick VIDEO_JUDGE_MODEL — gemini-2.5-flash-lite for cost (~$0.0072/240s clip) vs gemini-2.5-flash for quality. Free-tier flash-lite RPM argues for OFF-by-default.
plan.recommended_order:
Sprint A — product-visible quick wins, no ML, invariant-irrelevant (ship together): FE1 (embed bleed fix, best value/effort) -> FE2 (whitelist payload) -> GEN1 + GEN2 (metadata routing + EntertainmentAdapter = the minimal fix that stops the parody shipping 0 clips) -> FE3 (render title/card/watch-first/quality+flag chips) -> FE4 (mandatory rebuild into backend/static, surfacing GEN routing + all new fields).

Sprint B — the top quality item, invariant-critical (ship as one unit): CARD1 (judge-prompt fix, gated on kappa>=0.4) -> CARD2 (repair-terminal card-as-repair = the equations-clip/UAM rescue) -> CARD3 (post-snap/post-merge rescue + thread card into re-judge seams) -> CARD4 (unverified_kill=0 regression test + UAM fixture — MUST land with CARD2/CARD3).

Sprint C — cheap boundary + genre depth: BND1 (free text-only guards + kill fabricated terminators; attacks the dominant segmentation error) -> GEN3 (ensemble core: metadata + transcript-stats + middle sampling) -> GEN4 (Gemini-audio music tier, NOT inaSpeech) -> CARD5 (robust rescue seeding + suppression).

Sprint D — audio-gated + opt-in, MEASURE first (needs the golden set + audio-download confirmation): BND2 (faster-whisper re-time + cut-in-pause; measure residual) -> VID1 (generate_json_video enabler) -> VID2 (edge-probe, advisory) + VID4 (flags + eval plumbing) -> VID3 (render-audit, opt-in default OFF) -> GEN5 (reconciliation pass) -> ASR1 (parakeet opt-in/auto-trigger; defer default flip until golden-set A/B).

Cross-cutting prerequisite before Sprint D: build the small golden set. Do NOT start any torchaudio/inaSpeech/SaT work — all deferred or dropped.
